info.title: PSRule
info.description:
Validate infrastructure as code (IaC) and objects using PowerShell rules.

This project uses GitHub Issues to track bugs and feature requests. See GitHub project for more information.

hookCapture.patchTarget: Parse-postfix (OK: Command.Parse, OK: Command.Parse, OK: CommandLineParser.Parse, OK: CommandLineParser.Parse, OK: ParseResult.Invoke, OK: ParseErrorAction.Invoke, OK: HelpAction.Invoke, OK: ParseResult.InvokeAsync, OK: RootCommand.ctor(String))

options.name: --help
options.description: Show help and usage information

options.name: --version
options.description: Show version information

options.name: --option
options.description: Specifies the path to an options file. [default: ps-rule.yaml]

options.name: --verbose
options.description: Return verbose output. [default: False]

options.name: --debug
options.description: Return debug output. [default: False]

options.name: --path
options.description: The path to run commands within. By default, this is the current working directory.

commands.name: run
commands.description: Run rules against an input path and output the results.

options.name: --output-path
options.description: Specifies a path to write results to.

options.name: --output
options.description: Specifies the format to use when outputting results.

options.name: --input-path
options.description: The file or directory path to search for input file to use during a run. By default, this is the current working path.

options.name: --module
options.description: The name of one or more modules that contain rules or resources to use during a run.

options.name: --name
options.description: The name of one or more specific rules to run instead of all rules. By default, all rules are evaluated.

options.name: --baseline
options.description: The name of a specific baseline to use.

options.name: --formats
options.description: A list of formats to enable.

options.name: --outcome
options.description: Specifies the rule results to show in output. By default, Pass/ Fail/ Error results are shown.

options.name: --no-restore
options.description: Do not restore modules before running rules. [default: False]

options.name: --job-summary-path
options.description: The file path to write a job summary.

commands.name: module
commands.description: Manage or restore modules tracked by the module lock file and configured options.

commands.name: init
commands.description: Initialize a new or update an existing module lock file based on options.

options.name: --force
options.description: Force the creation of a new module lock file, even if one already exists. [default: False]

commands.name: list
commands.description: List any module and the installed versions from the lock file.

commands.name: add
commands.description: Add one or more modules to the lock file.

options.name: --version
options.description: The target version of the module to add.

options.name: --force
options.description: Override the module even if it already is added to the module list. [default: False]

options.name: --skip-verification
options.description: Skip verification of the module. [default: False]

options.name: --prerelease
options.description: Accept pre-release versions in addition to stable module versions. [default: False]

arguments.name: module
arguments.description: The name of one or more modules.

commands.name: remove
commands.description: Remove one or more modules from the lock file.

arguments.name: module
arguments.description: The name of one or more modules.

commands.name: upgrade
commands.description: Upgrade to the latest versions of any modules within the lock file.

options.name: --prerelease
options.description: Accept pre-release versions in addition to stable module versions. [default: False]

arguments.name: module
arguments.description: Optionally specifies one or more modules to apply to.

commands.name: restore
commands.description: Restore from the module lock file and configured options.

options.name: --force
options.description: Restore modules even when an existing version that meets constraints is already installed locally. [default: False]

commands.name: restore
commands.description: Restore from the module lock file and configured options. This is a shortcut for module restore.

options.name: --force
options.description: Restore modules even when an existing version that meets constraints is already installed locally. [default: False]
